  "findings": [
    "Shared neutral configuration causing AFCI nuisance tripping",
    "Improperly grouped or shared neutral conductors across different branch circuits"
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Trace and isolate the shared neutral connection",
    "Separate the neutral conductors for each individual AFCI-protected branch circuit",
    "Verify that each circuit has its own dedicated neutral wire back to the panel"
  ],
  "rationale": "An AFCI breaker detects current imbalances between the hot and neutral conductors. A shared neutral between two different circuits causes an imbalance that trips the AFCI. This wiring defect must be corrected by separating the neutrals before the AFCI can function properly and the installation can pass inspection.",
  "references": [
    "NEC 210.12",
    "NEC 200.4(A)"
  ]
}
